sections.subtitle: Match Overview
sections.text: CD Nacional will host Rio Ave at the Estadio da Madeira in Funchal for an intriguing clash. CD Nacional, currently sitting at 13th in the league with 33 points, will look to climb the table against Rio Ave, who are positioned 10th with 36 points. This matchup promises to be competitive given the relatively close standings of the two teams. The referee for the game, Bruno Jose Ribeiro Pires Costa, is known for averaging 4.4 yellow cards per game, which could influence the flow of this encounter. Both teams will aim to capitalize on their strengths in this crucial fixture.

sections.subtitle: Recent Head-to-Head History
sections.text: Looking at the last 5 matchups between these sides, Rio Ave holds the upper hand with 3 wins compared to CD Nacional’s 1 win and 1 draw. Their most recent encounter on December 29, 2024, saw Rio Ave triumph 2-1 at home, with Clayton earning the Player of the Match title with an impressive Sofascore rating of 8.7. Historically, Rio Ave seems to have the edge, but CD Nacional will be eager to turn the tide on their home turf. Joel Tagueu has been a standout for CD Nacional against Rio Ave in recent years, boasting an average Sofascore rating of 7.26 and scoring 4 goals in 4 starts.

sections.subtitle: CD Nacional’s Form and Key Players
sections.text: CD Nacional are in a tough spot, currently on a streak of 3 games without a win and without a clean sheet in their last 3 matches. They have also been the first to concede in 5 of their last 6 games, which could be a concern against a Rio Ave side with attacking threats. Among their key performers, Luís Esteves stands out as a midfielder ranked 2nd in the league for the percentage of long balls in total passes at 11.6%. Daniel Penha has contributed significantly with 4 goals, overperforming his expected goals of 2.98, while Isaac has chipped in with 3 goals. Defensively, Gustavo García has been solid with a team-high average rating of 7.11 and 3 assists.

sections.subtitle: Rio Ave’s Form and Star Players
sections.text: Rio Ave come into this game with a trend of matches featuring less than 2.5 goals in 5 of their last 7 outings, suggesting a tight defensive setup. Their games also tend to have fewer than 10.5 corners in all of their last 5 away matches, indicating a less open style of play. Leading their attack is Clayton, ranked 4th in the league for goals scored with 14, significantly overperforming his expected goals of 12.10. André Luiz, ranked 5th in the league for successful dribbles per game at 1.8, adds flair to their attack, while Demir Ege Tıknaz has impressed with 2 goals and 2 assists. Karem Zoabi has been in form over the last 5 matches, averaging a Sofascore rating of 7.5.

sections.subtitle: Team Statistics and Trends
sections.text: CD Nacional have scored 29 goals this season, ranking 14th in the league, and have conceded 44 goals, placing them 10th for least goals conceded. They excel in headers, ranking 5th with 8 goals from set-pieces, and are strong in fast breaks, ranked 3rd with 1.0625 per game. Rio Ave, on the other hand, have shown a balanced goal distribution across match periods, with 8 goals each in the 61-75 and 76-90 minute ranges, representing 17.6% of their total goals in each period. Both teams average around 4.4375 corners per game, with CD Nacional ranked 9th in the league for this metric, and their matches often see less than 10.5 corners, as evident in 7 of Nacional’s last 9 home games.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights
sections.text: CD Nacional’s strength lies in their ground duels, ranking 5th with a 50.88% win rate, and their ability to win aerial duels at 49.75%, ranked 8th. They also create penalty area entries at a decent rate of 27.65625 per game, ranking 7th in the league. However, their conversion rate of big chances is poor at 29.63%, placing them 17th. Rio Ave will likely rely on Clayton’s clinical finishing and André Luiz’s dribbling to break down Nacional’s defense. With Nacional often conceding first, Rio Ave might look to exploit early vulnerabilities and control possession, given Nacional’s 47.41% average ball possession, ranked 12th.

sections.subtitle: Interesting Facts
sections.text: CD Nacional rank 4th in the league for overperforming their expected goals, with a difference of 5.9881, showcasing their clinical nature despite a low total of 29 goals. They have hit the woodwork 9 times this season, ranking 8th among the unluckiest teams in the league. Rio Ave’s Clayton is not only a goal-scoring machine but also draws fouls frequently, averaging 2.7 per game, ranking 2nd in the league. CD Nacional’s Joel Tagueu, while not a high league-ranked player, remains a historical threat against Rio Ave with 4 goals in their last 5 encounters. Additionally, 65.52% of Nacional’s goals have been assisted, ranking 9th in the league for assisted goals percentage.
